site.name: FussyBean
site.tagline: Home coffee gear, by use case.

voice.tone: precise about specs, warm about the ritual, never snobbish; reads the way a good coffeeshop smells
voice.core_anxiety: spending real money on gear that won't actually improve the cup
framings: Upgrade-path: what to buy first, what to skip until later
Cross-brand honest comparison (Breville vs Gaggia vs De'Longhi vs Baratza)
Hardware not beans: we rank the machine, not the roast

default.name: Value
default.description: performance per dollar, total cost of ownership

pillars.label: Espresso Machines
pillars.blurb: Pump, lever, and super-auto machines for the home bar.

pillars.label: Grinders
pillars.blurb: Burr grinders that matter more than the machine.

pillars.label: Kettles & Scales
pillars.blurb: Gooseneck kettles, scales, pour-over, and press gear.

pillars.label: What It Costs
pillars.blurb: Cost-to-brew math, upgrade paths, and total-cost guides.

pillars.label: Gifts & Fun
pillars.blurb: Novelty and gift gear for the coffee-obsessed.

doctrineNotes: Comparison-and-fit framework. Hardware only, not beans (not yet). Never claim hands-on use; cite manufacturer specs, verified-buyer reviews, and independent testing (James Hoffmann, Whole Latte Love teardowns) as third-party evidence. No em dashes. No defensive audience exclusions.
